title: The Five Elements Theory — The Building Blocks of the Universe
summary: The Five Elements theory holds that everything in the universe is composed of Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water in dynamic interaction.
content:
The Five Elements theory (五行) is one of the foundational pillars of Chinese philosophy. It describes the dynamic relationships between five fundamental forces.

【The Five Elements】
- Wood (木): Growth, expansion, flexibility. Associated with spring, east, the liver.
- Fire (火): Heat, rising, transformation. Associated with summer, south, the heart.
- Earth (土): Nourishment, stability, centering. Associated with late summer, center, the spleen.
- Metal (金): Contraction, structure, clarity. Associated with autumn, west, the lungs.
- Water (水): Stillness, depth, adaptability. Associated with winter, north, the kidneys.

【Generating Cycle (Sheng)】
Each element nourishes and supports the next:
Wood feeds Fire → Fire creates Earth (ash) → Earth bears Metal (ore) → Metal collects Water (condensation) → Water nourishes Wood.

【Controlling Cycle (Ke)】
Each element restrains another to maintain balance:
Wood parts Earth (roots) → Earth dams Water → Water extinguishes Fire → Fire melts Metal → Metal cuts Wood.

【Clinical Applications】
In traditional Chinese medicine, the Five Elements theory is used to diagnose and treat imbalances in the body. For example, a person with excessive Wood (anger, liver issues) might be treated by strengthening Metal (lungs, grief), which controls Wood.

title: The Five Elements Correspondence System
summary: The Five Elements create a comprehensive web of correspondences linking nature, the body, and human experience.
content:
One of the most fascinating aspects of Five Elements theory is its system of correspondences, linking everything in the universe into a unified framework.

【Correspondence Table】

Direction: Wood(East) Fire(South) Earth(Center) Metal(West) Water(North)
Season: Wood(Spring) Fire(Summer) Earth(Late Summer) Metal(Autumn) Water(Winter)
Color: Wood(Green) Fire(Red) Earth(Yellow) Metal(White) Water(Black)
Taste: Wood(Sour) Fire(Bitter) Earth(Sweet) Metal(Spicy) Water(Salty)
Organ: Wood(Liver) Fire(Heart) Earth(Spleen) Metal(Lung) Water(Kidney)
Sense: Wood(Eyes) Fire(Tongue) Earth(Mouth) Metal(Nose) Water(Ears)
Emotion: Wood(Anger) Fire(Joy) Earth(Worry) Metal(Grief) Water(Fear)
Virtue: Wood(Benevolence) Fire(Ritual) Earth(Trust) Metal(Righteousness) Water(Wisdom)

【Heavenly Stems & Five Elements】
Jia-Yi = Wood, Bing-Ding = Fire, Wu-Ji = Earth, Geng-Xin = Metal, Ren-Gui = Water

【Practical Application】
The five-element correspondence system embodies the ancient Chinese concept of "Heaven and Humanity as One" (天人合一). Understanding these relationships helps in fields ranging from medicine to feng shui to personal development.
